meaning: n.(尤指在公共场所的)吵架,打架
examples: I was responsible for creating a huge affray.
That is common affray, his wife blame he comes home too late.
The crime of Affray is a common and frequently occurring crime.
In the early 1990s, he was convicted of criminal damage, affray and driving with no insurance.
Twenty arrests were made for public order offences, affray, assault, theft, criminal damage and possessing weapons.
